title: Site-wide navigation — IA + canonical pattern + 4 anchor pages

summary.nav_pattern: Non-sticky header strip (48px) with wordmark + category label strip (desktop-only, decorative) + single 'All of Stratum' disclosure button that opens one full-width 6-column index panel. One aria-expanded, one ESC handler, one focus-return. Mobile: hamburger opens full-page overlay with 6 native <details> accordion sections.

o1_status.details: WCAG 2.2 AAA keyboard contract implemented: Tab order matches DOM order (skip → site-nav → rt-stamp → section nav), aria-expanded on disclosure button, aria-controls pointing to panel ID, panel uses HTML hidden attribute (not class toggle), ESC closes and returns focus, no keyboard trap (Tab exits panel naturally), target sizes ≥44px on all interactive elements, focus-visible rings on every focusable element using ochre outline, aria-current='page' on active links per page, role=navigation aria-label='Site' distinct from existing nav landmarks, prefers-reduced-motion respected (transitions only under no-preference), aria-label on hamburger and close button. Skip-link added to product/index.html which was missing one. Mobile overlay uses role=dialog aria-modal=true with body scroll lock.
new_findings: product/index.html was missing a skip-link entirely — fixed in this dispatch
Ochre column heads (#b8651f on #f4ecda) contrast is 3.8:1 — passes AA for large text but not AAA for normal text; flagged in navigation.html §6 for next design audit
existing_closed: No site-wide navigation existed — visitors had no way to discover the IA — now resolved for anchor pages
